title: HTTP/1.1 Message Framing and Length Precedence · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs http/1.1 message framing and length precedence from bounded byte and state evidence, challenges one misconception, and revises the RFC 9112 message-body length decision table. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for RFC 9112 message-body length decision table after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Apply the RFC 9112 message-body length precedence in order using request or response role, method, status, transfer coding, content length, and connection closure.
Forbid content framing for HEAD responses, 1xx, 204, successful CONNECT, and other context-specific cases while retaining metadata meaning.
Validate transfer-coding order, require final chunked coding for request framing, and close after ambiguous Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length input.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Body Length Precedence
steps.instruction:
Order the Body Length Precedence evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Body Length Precedence.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Body Length Precedence assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Body Length Precedence, keep Body Length Precedence connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.text: A review protocol team handles case 4317 in a canary edge service advertising HTTP/3 with bounded fallback. Build deterministic Go evidence to apply framing precedence and close on Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length ambiguity; preserve this operating constraint: all attacker-controlled counts must be validated before allocation or slicing; then split every structural delimiter across a different read and retain the same result. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Apply the RFC 9112 message-body length precedence in order using request or response role, method, status, transfer coding, content length, and connection closure.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Body Length Precedence has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record framing source, expected bytes, observed bytes, completion, residual buffer, close requirement, and rejection reason.

hints: For Record what required effort · Review Framing Completeness Evidence, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Framing Completeness Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Record what required effort · Review Framing Completeness Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A review protocol team handles case 4324 in a municipal gateway receiving one-byte TCP fragments. Build deterministic Go evidence to apply framing precedence and close on Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length ambiguity; preserve this operating constraint: all attacker-controlled counts must be validated before allocation or slicing; then lower one byte or time budget and prove rejection occurs before allocation or dispatch. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Record framing source, expected bytes, observed bytes, completion, residual buffer, close requirement, and rejection reason.

For Record what required effort · Review Framing Completeness Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A valid start line and field section prove the complete message is safe to dispatch.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The decision applies Body Length Precedence to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Body Length Precedence.

checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about No Content Responses.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using record and framing plus specific case evidence.
